verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a single-pass TM protein with its N-terminus in the ER lumen and C-terminus in the cytosol; its dominant residence is the ER membrane. However, STIM1 has a documented plasma-membrane pool: a fraction is delivered to the PM with N-terminus extracellular, and surface STIM1 has been detected by surface biotinylation, flow cytometry, and anti-STIM1 antibodies on intact cells (notably platelets and some cancer cells). This minority surface pool with its own TM anchor qualifies as dual localization. Not yes (ER is dominant compartment); not no (documented surface pool rules out endomembrane_resident-only).
predicted_key_uncertainty: Magnitude and generality of the PM-resident STIM1 pool across tissues is debated; some reports attribute surface signal to specific cell types (platelets, tumor cells).
